lostDestinations.source: Sunshine Skies AHN history; service ended May 23 2008
Sunshine Skies AHN history; ASA Delta Connection ended Athens ~early 1985
Sunshine Skies AHN history; CCAir ceased operations 2002 replaced by Air Midwest

note: CLT appears twice: first served by Piedmont then CCAir/USAir Express through 2002, then Air Midwest/US Airways Express ended May 23 2008; BNA SeaPort Airlines EAS ended Sep 30 2014; ATL service also lost ~1985 when ASA Delta Connection ended

lostDestinations.source: Georgia DOT Middle Georgia Regional Airport report Sep 2020; enplanements fell from 34500 in 2004 to 10029 in 2008 after Delta dropped service
Search result: USAir Express discontinued service ~1991 same time Eastern folded
Search result: Silver Airways 2013-2014 offered brief routes including to Orlando before ceasing service 2014

detail: Delta Connection (ASA) served Macon from 1980s to October 2007 peaking at 34,500 enplanements in 2004; airport went without commercial service 2014-2017 until EAS restored by Contour Airlines now flying to Washington Dulles (IAD) as of May 2025.
source: Georgia DOT Middle Georgia Regional Airport report Sep 2020; airserviceone.com May 2025

lostDestinations.source: Sunshine Skies Columbus GA history: American Eagle ended six years of service October 1995 citing low traffic
Sunshine Skies Columbus GA history: Northwest Airlink nonstop Memphis ended mid-1990s
Sunshine Skies Columbus GA history: American Eagle served Nashville and Charlotte until October 1995
Sunshine Skies Columbus GA history: Delta mainline Columbus as stop on Atlanta-Fort Worth route ended ~1979 after deregulation
Search result: American Eagle CLT/DFW service Aug 2021-Apr 2023 ended citing pilot shortage; ATL only destination now

detail: Columbus lost mainline service from Eastern (1979) and Southern (1979) after deregulation, then American Eagle service to CLT/BNA (1995) and Northwest Airlink to MEM; American Eagle briefly returned Aug 2021-Apr 2023 before withdrawing again, leaving only Delta to ATL.
source: Sunshine Skies Columbus GA history (parts 1-3); flycolumbusga.com

lostDestinations.source: Sunshine Skies ABY history: Republic/Delta Connection ATL service continuous since 1979; currently retains ATL only
Sunshine Skies ABY history: Republic Oct 1982 timetable 5 flights/day ATL and 2 to Valdosta; Valdosta leg ended as Republic wound down
Sunshine Skies ABY history: Republic operated Albany-Montgomery pairing before network contraction mid-1980s

detail: Albany lost Eastern Airlines in 1971 and Air South in 1972; Republic/Northwest consolidated to Atlanta-only service by late 1980s; Southwest Georgia Regional Airport now served exclusively by Delta Connection to ATL with ~40,000 annual operations.
source: Sunshine Skies Albany GA history (sunshineskies.com/aby.html); airport.albanyga.gov

lostDestinations.source: AOPA/FlightAware VLD: sole current route is Delta Connection to ATL daily
Sunshine Skies ABY: Republic operated Albany-Valdosta pairing into early 1980s before contraction

detail: Valdosta lost multi-city Republic Airlines network after Southern-North Central merger (1979) and subsequent route consolidation; airport now serves only Delta Connection to Atlanta daily using CRJ-700 aircraft.
source: dot.ga.gov Valdosta Regional Airport report Sep 2020; AOPA airport details VLD

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia BQK: ASA began 1981 nonstop ATL; Endeavor took over, upgraded to CRJ-700/900 in 2023 — ATL only route retained

detail: Atlantic Southeast Airlines began ATL service in 1981 and code-shared as Delta Connection from 1984; prior to that Delta served nearby McKinnon St. Simons Island Airport (1940s–1960s); today only ATL via Endeavor Air remains.
source: Wikipedia Brunswick Golden Isles Airport; search result summary

lostDestinations.source: City of Ocala airport history; Skyway reduced to 2 daily Orlando roundtrips by 1986, final flight June 30 1987
Sunshine Skies Ocala history: Skyway expanded to Miami then traffic declined through mid-1980s
Sunshine Skies Ocala: Skyway contracted for Ocala-Gainesville-Tampa, expanded then cut back
Sunshine Skies Ocala: Eastern served JAX-GNV-OCF-VRB-MIA routing; last Electra left Ocala 1972
Sunshine Skies Ocala: Eastern Convair/Electra JAX-GNV-OCF-VRB-MIA ended 1972

detail: Eastern Air Lines served Ocala from 1950 to 1972 on a Jacksonville–Miami multi-stop route; five commuter carriers operated 1971–1987, the last being Skyway of Ocala/Allegheny Commuter ending June 30 1987 with only 2 passengers per Orlando flight; no scheduled service since.
source: City of Ocala airport history (ocalafl.gov); Sunshine Skies Ocala history; WUFT 2020 report

lostDestinations.source: Sunshine Skies Marathon history: American Eagle ended service Spring 2000 as final major code-share at MTH
Sunshine Skies Marathon history pt.2: ASA daily nonstop ATL-MTH began Feb 15 2007, Delta's first scheduled jet service to MTH, ended Sep 2007 after <7 months
Sunshine Skies / keysnews.com: Continental contracted Cape Air RSW-MTH Dec 2008; contract ended Apr 29 2009 not renewed
Sunshine Skies Marathon history: commuter routes to Tampa via PBA/Air Sunshine through 1990s, ended ~1999-2000

detail: Marathon had scheduled service from 1959 (National Airlines flag stop) through 2009; American Eagle provided final code-share service until Spring 2000; Delta tried nonstop ATL service in 2007 for <7 months; Cape Air/Continental Connection was last carrier, ending April 29 2009.
source: Sunshine Skies history of Marathon airport (parts 1-2); Monroe County FAQ; keysnews.com

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Naples Airport FL: American Eagle Miami service ended 2001
Wikipedia Naples Airport FL: US Airways Express Tampa service ended 2003
Wikipedia Naples Airport FL: ASA Delta Connection ATL-APF 2004-2007, city revenue guarantee ran out
Search result: Elite Airways launched Feb 2016 Portland ME/Newark/Vero Beach/Melbourne; ceased Mar 2017
Search result: Elite Airways Portland ME-Naples service ended Mar 2017

detail: Naples peaked at 195,000 passengers in 1980 before nearby Southwest Florida International (RSW) opened; American Eagle, US Airways Express, and Delta Connection all withdrew 2001-2007; Elite Airways made the final attempt Feb 2016-Mar 2017, ending all scheduled service.
source: Wikipedia Naples Airport (Florida); latitude.to coordinates; simpleflying.com Naples airport guide

lostDestinations.source: flynf.com airport history: Frontier launched Trenton May 2014, expanded to PHL/ORD 2016, suspended all service 2017
flynf.com airport history: Via Air CLT-UST Dec 18 2014 through Nov 2018
Search result: Skybus served UST from CMH, PSM, GSO, RIC, CHI; filed Ch.11 bankruptcy 2008

detail: St. Augustine only had sporadic service: Skybus 2007-2008, Via Air to Charlotte 2014-2018, and Frontier 2014-2017; currently served only by Elite Airways to Newark/EWR and Portland/PWM with a hiatus 2018-2021.
source: flynf.com Airport History; aeroxplorer.com airport snapshot UST

lostDestinations.source: Search result / en-academic Daytona Beach: Eastern operated wide-body jets DAB-LGA in 1976; Eastern bankrupt 1991
Search result: American mainline 2-3 daily DAB-RDU while AA had RDU hub; ended 1994 when AA closed RDU hub
Search result: Eastern served DAB-MIA among other hub cities; service ended with Eastern bankruptcy 1991
Search result: Eastern 1976 nonstop DAB to multiple major cities including Chicago; ended 1991

detail: Daytona Beach had extensive mainline jet service from Eastern (1970s-1991) and American (1989-1994 RDU hub), plus Delta and Continental; currently retains American to CLT/DFW and Delta to ATL but lost all non-hub mainline routes.
source: Daytona Beach International Airport flydaytonafirst.com historical info; en-academic.com article

lostDestinations.source: Search result: Eastern served GNV from 1950s with ATL and MIA routes; bankruptcy ended service 1991
Search result: Eastern nonstop ATL and MIA from GNV by 1970s; ended with Eastern bankruptcy Jan 1991
Search result: Air Florida served GNV with MIA/TPA/OCF routes late 1970s-early 1980s before collapse

detail: Gainesville lost Eastern Airlines' longtime service (ATL, MIA, TPA routes) in 1991; Air Florida collapsed 1984; airport retains American and Delta to CLT/DFW/ATL hubs but no longer has nonstop service to New York or major non-hub cities it held in Eastern era.
source: Airport History page gra-gnv.com; search result summaries
